quiz.question:
Black threatens ...Rb2+ and ...Ra2+ perpetual, with an attack on our queen. 

How should White avoid a draw?

answers.text: Keep the queen defended on the second rank (Qe2), we don’t mind if the rook captures our queen.

feedback.text: Exactly! We are up so much material that we can afford to trade the queen for our opponent's rook and win later.

distractors.text: We should save our queen, play Qe3 and threaten Black's c3-pawn.

feedback.text: Remember, Black has perpetual with ...Rb2+ and ...Ra2+!

distractors.text: Let's capture the rook. We are up so much material that we'll be able to win anyway.

feedback.text:
This allows Black to get a dangerous passed pawn after ...cxd2, recapturing our queen. 

And since they have a light-squared bishop, we won't be able to stop the c-pawn.
